content:
# Numbers Chapter 31 — The PNEUMA Bible (Dynamic)
**Translator:** PNEUMA | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 186-188 | **Source Image:** `NUM_031_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_031_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_031_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0137-pa_0138

---

**1** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**2** "Exact vengeance for the sons of Israel against the Midianites. After that, you will be gathered to your people."

**3** And Moses spoke to the people, saying, "Arm men from among you for war, and let them go against Midian to carry out YHWH's vengeance against Midian.

**4** One thousand from each tribe, from all the tribes of Israel, you shall send to war."

**5** And so there were mustered from the thousands of Israel one thousand per tribe — twelve thousand armed for battle.

**6** And Moses sent them to war, one thousand from each tribe, along with Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, who carried the holy vessels and the trumpets for sounding the alarm.

**7** And they waged war against Midian, just as YHWH had commanded Moses, and they killed every male.

**8** And they killed the kings of Midian along with those already slain — Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba — the five kings of Midian. And Balaam son of Beor they also put to the sword.

**9** And the sons of Israel took captive the women of Midian and their little ones, and they plundered all their cattle, all their flocks, and all their wealth.

**10** And all their cities where they had settled and all their encampments they burned with fire.

**11** And they took all the spoil and all the plunder, both of people and of livestock.

**12** And they brought the captives, the plunder, and the spoil to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the congregation of the sons of Israel, at the camp on the plains of Moab, which are by the Jordan across from Jericho.

**13** And Moses and Eleazar the priest and all the leaders of the congregation went out to meet them outside the camp.

**14** And Moses was furious with the officers of the army — the commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds — who were returning from the military campaign.

**15** And Moses said to them, "You let all the women live?

**16** Look — these are the very ones who, on Balaam's advice, caused the sons of Israel to act unfaithfully against YHWH in the affair of Peor, so that the plague struck YHWH's congregation!

**17** Now therefore, kill every male among the little ones, and kill every woman who has known a man by lying with him.

**18** But all the young girls who have not known a man by lying with him — keep alive for yourselves.

**19** And you — camp outside the camp for seven days. Whoever has killed a person, and whoever has touched a slain body, purify yourselves on the third day and on the seventh day — both you and your captives.

**20** And every garment, every article of leather, everything made of goat hair, and every article of wood — you shall purify."

**21** And Eleazar the priest said to the men of war who had gone into battle, "This is the statute of the law that YHWH commanded Moses:

**22** Only the gold, the silver, the bronze, the iron, the tin, and the lead —

**23** everything that can withstand fire — you shall pass through fire, and it will be clean; nevertheless it must also be purified with the water of purification. And everything that cannot withstand fire you shall pass through water.

**24** And you shall wash your garments on the seventh day and be clean, and afterward you may come into the camp."

**25** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,

**26** "Take a count of the plunder that was captured, both of people and of livestock — you, Eleazar the priest, and the heads of the ancestral houses of the congregation.

**27** And divide the plunder into two parts: between the warriors who went out to battle and the entire congregation.

**28** And levy a tribute for YHWH from the men of war who went out to battle: one life out of every five hundred, from the people, from the cattle, from the donkeys, and from the flocks.

**29** Take it from their half and give it to Eleazar the priest as a contribution to YHWH.

**30** And from the half belonging to the sons of Israel, you shall take one portion out of every fifty — from the people, from the cattle, from the donkeys, from the flocks, from all the livestock — and give them to the Levites who keep charge of the tabernacle of YHWH."

**31** And Moses and Eleazar the priest did just as YHWH had commanded Moses.

**32** And the plunder — what remained of the spoil that the men of war had taken — was six hundred and seventy-five thousand sheep,

**33** and seventy-two thousand cattle,

**34** and sixty-one thousand donkeys,

**35** and thirty-two thousand persons in all — women who had not known a man by lying with him.

**36** And the half — the portion of those who had gone out to war — was in number: three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,

**37** and YHWH's tribute from the sheep was six hundred and seventy-five.

**38** And the cattle were thirty-six thousand, and YHWH's tribute from them was seventy-two.

**39** And the donkeys were thirty thousand five hundred, and YHWH's tribute from them was sixty-one.

**40** And the persons were sixteen thousand, and YHWH's tribute from them was thirty-two persons.

**41** And Moses gave the tribute — YHWH's contribution — to Eleazar the priest, just as YHWH had commanded Moses.

**42** And from the half belonging to the sons of Israel, which Moses had divided from the men who had gone to war —

**43** now the congregation's half was three hundred and thirty-seven thousand five hundred sheep,

**44** and thirty-six thousand cattle,

**45** and thirty thousand five hundred donkeys,

**46** and sixteen thousand persons —

**47** from this half belonging to the sons of Israel, Moses took one out of every fifty, both of persons and of livestock, and gave them to the Levites who kept charge of the tabernacle of YHWH, just as YHWH had commanded Moses.

**48** And the officers who were over the thousands of the army — the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds — approached Moses.

**49** And they said to Moses, "Your servants have taken a count of the men of war who are under our command, and not one man is missing from among us.

**50** So we have brought YHWH's offering — what each man found: articles of gold, armlets, bracelets, signet rings, earrings, and pendants — to make atonement for ourselves before YHWH."

**51** And Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from them — all of it crafted articles.

**52** And all the gold of the contribution that they set apart for YHWH was sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels, from the commanders of thousands and the commanders of hundreds.

**53** (The men of war had each plundered for himself.)

**54** And Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds, and they brought it into the tent of meeting as a memorial for the sons of Israel before YHWH.

---

## PNEUMA's Meaning Notes

**Verse 2 — "Exact vengeance" (נְקֹם נִקְמַת):** The Hebrew uses a cognate accusative construction (nqm niqmat), an intensifying figure that conveys the gravity of the act. This is not private revenge but divinely authorized retribution for the Baal Peor incident (Numbers 25). The Vaticanus renders this with ekdikesis, confirming the sense of judicially enacted vengeance.

**Verse 6 — "Holy vessels and the trumpets" (כְּלֵי הַקֹּדֶשׁ וַחֲצֹצְרוֹת הַתְּרוּעָה):** The "holy vessels" likely refers to the Ark or sacred objects carried into battle as a sign of YHWH's presence. The trumpets (chatsotsrot) are the silver signal trumpets of Numbers 10:1-10, distinct from the shofar. Phinehas leads the campaign not as military commander but as priestly representative, underscoring that this war is ritual and sacred in character.

**Verse 8 — Balaam's death:** Balaam, who had blessed Israel in chapters 22-24, is here executed with the Midianite kings. Verse 16 explains why: he had counseled Midian to use their women to entice Israel into idolatry at Peor (cf. Num 25:1-9; 31:16). His death closes the Balaam narrative arc that began in chapter 22.

**Verse 14 — "Moses was furious" (וַיִּקְצֹף מֹשֶׁה):** The verb qatsaph denotes intense, burning anger. Moses's rage at the officers is not arbitrary cruelty but alarm that sparing the Midianite women would replicate the exact conditions of the Peor disaster. The Vaticanus uses orgizo, confirming wrathful indignation.

**Verse 17-18 — The killing order:** This is among the most morally difficult passages in the Torah. Moses commands the execution of male children and women who have been sexually active, sparing only virgin girls. The rationale within the narrative logic is cultic purity: those who participated in or were born from the Peor seduction are considered vectors of apostasy. Modern readers should not flatten the moral difficulty of this text.

**Verse 19 — Corpse impurity (טָמֵא):** Contact with a dead body rendered a person ritually impure for seven days (cf. Num 19). Even a divinely commanded war produces impurity. The theology here is significant: killing, even when sanctioned, is still contaminating. The warriors must undergo the same purification as anyone who touches a corpse.

**Verses 22-23 — Purification by fire and water:** Eleazar provides practical halakhah for purifying plundered goods. Metal objects that can withstand heat are passed through fire; everything else goes through water. Both must additionally be sprinkled with the "water of purification" (mei niddah, the red heifer mixture of Num 19). The Vaticanus text aligns closely here, using hydor hagnismou.

**Verse 28 — "One life out of every five hundred" (אֶחָד נֶפֶשׁ מֵחֲמֵשׁ הַמֵּאוֹת):** The tribute ratio differs between the warriors (1:500) and the congregation (1:50, v.30). The warriors, having risked their lives, give a smaller percentage to YHWH via the priests. The congregation, who stayed behind, gives a larger share to the Levites. This graduated system balances equity with recognition of sacrifice.

**Verse 49 — "Not one man is missing":** This is remarkable and presented as miraculous. Twelve thousand men fought a war and returned without a single casualty. The officers' spontaneous gold offering (v.50) is their response of gratitude — an atonement offering not for sin but for the astonishing preservation of life.

**Verse 50 — Gold ornaments:** The Hebrew lists specific jewelry types: 'ets'adah (armlet), tsamid (bracelet), tabba'at (signet ring), 'agil (earring), kumaz (pendant or ornamental bead). These were personal spoils taken from the Midianites. The officers voluntarily surrender them as sacred tribute.

**Verse 52 — Sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels:** At roughly 11.5 grams per shekel, this amounts to approximately 192 kilograms (423 pounds) of gold — an enormous offering. This sum comes only from the officers; the common soldiers kept their personal plunder (v.53).

**Verse 54 — "A memorial before YHWH" (זִכָּרוֹן לִבְנֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל לִפְנֵי יהוה):** The gold is placed in the tent of meeting as a zikkaron — a physical reminder of YHWH's protection. This parallels other memorial offerings (cf. Exod 30:16). The chapter ends not with the violence of war but with worship and gratitude.

**Structural note:** Numbers 31 serves as the final military narrative before Israel's entry into the land. It resolves the Midian/Peor crisis of chapter 25, executes judgment on Balaam from chapters 22-24, and transitions toward the land-distribution and boundary texts of chapters 32-36. The elaborate enumeration of plunder and its distribution reflects priestly concern with order, fairness, and the consecration of all things — even the aftermath of war — to YHWH.
